content.text: I’ll locate the STL bootstrap definitions for vector registration to see where copy-only operations are being instantiated.

I’ll track the fix as a few concrete steps: reproduce the compile failure with a focused test, update STL bootstrap registration to avoid copy-only operations for move-only vector value types, validate, then remove the temporary test.

content.content:
1	// This file is distributed under the BSD License.
2	// See "license.txt" for details.
3	// Copyright 2009-2012, Jonathan Turner (jonathan@emptycrate.com)
4	// Copyright 2009-2018, Jason Turner (jason@emptycrate.com)
5	// http://www.chaiscript.com
6	
7	// This is an open source non-commercial project. Dear PVS-Studio, please check it.
8	// PVS-Studio Static Code Analyzer for C, C++ and C#: http://www.viva64.com
9	
10	#ifndef CHAISCRIPT_PROXY_CONSTRUCTORS_HPP_
11	#define CHAISCRIPT_PROXY_CONSTRUCTORS_HPP_
12	
13	#include "proxy_functions.hpp"
14	
15	namespace chaiscript::dispatch::detail {
16	  template<typename Class, typename... Params>
17	  Proxy_Function build_constructor_(Class (*)(Params...)) {
18	    if constexpr (!std::is_copy_constructible_v<Class>) {
19	      auto call = [](auto &&...param) { return std::make_shared<Class>(std::forward<decltype(param)>(param)...); };
20	
21	      return Proxy_Function(
22	          chaiscript::make_shared<dispatch::Proxy_Function_Base,
23	                                  dispatch::Proxy_Function_Callable_Impl<std::shared_ptr<Class>(Params...), decltype(call)>>(call));
24	    } else if constexpr (true) {
25	      auto call = [](auto &&...param) { return Class(std::forward<decltype(param)>(param)...); };
26	
27	      return Proxy_Function(
28	          chaiscript::make_shared<dispatch::Proxy_Function_Base, dispatch::Proxy_Function_Callable_Impl<Class(Params...), decltype(call)>>(
29	              call));
30	    }
31	  }
32	} // namespace chaiscript::dispatch::detail
33	
34	namespace chaiscript {
35	  /// \brief Generates a constructor function for use with ChaiScript
36	  ///
37	  /// \tparam T The signature of the constructor to generate. In the form of: ClassType (ParamType1, ParamType2, ...)
38	  ///
39	  /// Example:
40	  /// \code
41	  ///    chaiscript::ChaiScript chai;
42	  ///    // Create a new function that creates a MyClass object using the (int, float) constructor
43	  ///    // and call that function "MyClass" so that it appears as a normal constructor to the user.
44	  ///    chai.add(constructor<MyClass (int, float)>(), "MyClass");
45	  /// \endcode
46	  template<typename T>
47	  Proxy_Function constructor() {
48	    T *f = nullptr;
49	    return (dispatch::detail::build_constructor_(f));
50	  }
51	} // namespace chaiscript
52	
53	#endif
54

todos.content: Create a focused compile test for vector of unique_ptr registration

content.text:
Implemented the fix in `include/chaiscript/dispatchkit/bootstrap_stl.hpp`.

Summary:
- Added type-trait gating for STL container registrations that require copyable elements.
- `vector_type<std::vector<std::unique_ptr<T>>>` can now instantiate without trying to register operations that copy `unique_ptr`.
- Copy-dependent operations are skipped when the container/value type is not copy-capable:
  - copy constructor / assignment registration
  - `push_back(const value_type&)`
  - `push_front(const_reference)`
  - `insert_at`
  - resize-with-value
- Default resize is only registered when the value type is default-constructible.
- Non-copying operations like indexing, front/back access, pop, erase, reserve, size, range support, etc. remain available.

Validation performed:
- Created a temporary compile test registering `std::vector<std::unique_ptr<Foo>>`.
- Verified it now compiles and runs.
- Ran existing `compiled_tests`; all passed:
  - `216 assertions in 59 test cases`

Temporary test files were removed. No commits were made.

result:
Implemented the fix in `include/chaiscript/dispatchkit/bootstrap_stl.hpp`.

Summary:
- Added type-trait gating for STL container registrations that require copyable elements.
- `vector_type<std::vector<std::unique_ptr<T>>>` can now instantiate without trying to register operations that copy `unique_ptr`.
- Copy-dependent operations are skipped when the container/value type is not copy-capable:
  - copy constructor / assignment registration
  - `push_back(const value_type&)`
  - `push_front(const_reference)`
  - `insert_at`
  - resize-with-value
- Default resize is only registered when the value type is default-constructible.
- Non-copying operations like indexing, front/back access, pop, erase, reserve, size, range support, etc. remain available.

Validation performed:
- Created a temporary compile test registering `std::vector<std::unique_ptr<Foo>>`.
- Verified it now compiles and runs.
- Ran existing `compiled_tests`; all passed:
  - `216 assertions in 59 test cases`

Temporary test files were removed. No commits were made.
